title: 1A: N/S FLOW
subject: DIRECTIVE 82-A: N/S AXIS ALIGNMENT
body: Latency on SEC-082 N/S corridor exceeds acceptable thresholds. East/West sensors are temporarily disabled. Establish baseline throughput.

title: 1B: E/W FLOW
subject: DIRECTIVE 82-B: E/W AXIS ALIGNMENT
body: N/S sensors have been taken offline for diagnostics. East/West traffic is now backing up into the industrial district. Restore flow without exceeding cycle budget.
bullets: Ensure both East and West axes are processed.
Keep East and West traffic isolated to prevent incidents.

title: 1C: FULL INTEGRATION
subject: DIRECTIVE 82-C: FULL OPERATION
body: Diagnostics complete. All sensors on SEC-082 are active. Congestion is critical. Design a 4-phase cycle to process all directions efficiently. Do not exceed the 60-tick cycle overheat limit.
bullets: Ensure all four axes maintain minimum flow.
Isolate conflicting movements into discrete phases.

title: 1D: PROTECTED LEFTS
subject: DIRECTIVE 82-D: LEFT TURN CONFLICT
body: Collision data suggests Northbound drivers are failing to yield. Implement a dedicated Left-Turn phase to isolate them from Southbound oncoming traffic.
bullets: Ensure North-Left and South-Left have dedicated time.
initialCode:
phase(1):
    NORTH_STRAIGHT.GO
    SOUTH_STRAIGHT.GO

phase(2):
    # Add Left Turn Logic here

title: 2A: THE RUSH HOUR
subject: DIRECTIVE 83-A: ASYMMETRIC LOAD
body: Morning commute in effect. Northbound volume is 3x higher than other directions. Adjust phase timings to favor the heavy load.
bullets: Do not allow Southbound to stall.

title: 2B: INDUSTRIAL REPAIR
subject: DIRECTIVE 83-B: THRU-LANE CLOSURE
body: Utility repairs have blocked all 'Straight' lanes on the East/West axis. Only turning movements are possible. Route vehicles through side streets.
bullets: Only Left and Right turns are active for E/W.

title: 2C: THE PEDESTRIAN BRIDGE
subject: DIRECTIVE 83-C: CROSSWALK PRIORITY
body: The local schools are out. High pedestrian volume requires a dedicated 'All-Walk' phase where all vehicle movement is halted.

title: 3A: PERMISSIVE FLOW
subject: DIRECTIVE 84-A: THE YIELD PROTOCOL
body: Efficiency is low. Instead of strict green lights, use .YIELD commands for right turns and left turns to allow movement during gaps in traffic.
bullets: Utilize .YIELD for at least two movements.

title: 3B: SENSOR ACTIVATION
subject: DIRECTIVE 84-B: DYNAMIC ROUTING
body: Your hardware has been upgraded with queue sensors. Use 'if' statements to trigger special phases only when specific lanes are backed up.
bullets: Reduce hardware overheating by minimizing active phases.

title: 3C: THE SPLIT PHASE
subject: DIRECTIVE 84-C: HEAVY TRUCK CORRIDOR
body: Large trucks from the West are causing accidents when turning. Implement a 'Split Phase' where West goes entirely (Left, Straight, Right) followed by East.
bullets: Isolate WB flow from EB flow entirely.

title: 4A: THE DIAMOND
subject: DIRECTIVE 85-A: 8-PHASE CHALLENGE
body: Critical congestion. All lanes are active. All volumes are high. You must design a perfect 8-phase rotation or the system will crash from overflow.
bullets: System overflow will occur at 15 vehicles in queue.

title: 4B: EMERGENCY CORRIDOR
subject: DIRECTIVE 85-B: HOSPITAL ACCESS
body: The Southbound corridor is now an emergency route. It must remain clear. Use sensors to purge Southbound queues the moment they exceed 2 vehicles.
bullets: Southbound queue must stay below 5 at all times.

title: SANDBOX
subject: SEC-082 SIMULATION ENVIRONMENT
body: Freeplay environment enabled. All sensors and overrides are unlocked. Use this simulation to test various traffic profiles.
